mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals expanding consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS monthly change on 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window, or the decile spread in forward returns between periods of rising vs falling RSAFS is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) increases by 30 basis points or more over a three-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that erode consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the median 20-day forward return is not negative, or the proportion of positive returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated (above 4.5%) and housing starts (HOUST) are declining month-over-month, the combined tightening from policy and weakening housing demand amplifies economic downside, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing downturn
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the following 20-day S&P 500 return has a win rate above 45% or average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market contraction phase (HOUST declining from its 12-month peak), continued declines in housing starts further depress residential investment and related sectors, weighing on GDP and corporate profits, resulting in negative forward equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is at least 10% below its 12-month high
failure_condition: In periods when HOUST is declining from peak, the 20-day forward return distribution is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10) or the mean return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 0.4% signals rising inflation, which pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain or tighten policy, thereby discounting equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following such inflation spikes is not negative, or the absolute IC of the trigger indicator on forward returns is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above trend and retail sales (RSAFS) also expand month-over-month, the joint signal of a strengthening labor market and robust consumer demand reinforces positive economic momentum, which drives S&P 500 returns positively over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 100,000 and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0%
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the 60-day forward return is negative more often than not, or the rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In the late-cycle expansion phase, industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits slowing growth; a sequential decline in INDPRO over two months indicates that the economy is losing momentum, which often precedes equity market corrections over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change is negative for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The average 20-day return following two consecutive INDPRO declines is positive, or the frequency of positive returns exceeds 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects the broad health of the economy; sustained positive growth signals healthy corporate earnings conditions, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly (60 trading day) horizon, irrespective of short-term fluctuations.
failure_condition: The quarterly forward return following GDPC1 releases is not positive on average over a 5-year rolling window, or the correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is insignificant.
